```js
interface CssRule {
  selector: string; // text before '{', trimmed
  declarations: string; // text between { and matching }
}

function parseRules(css: string): CssRule[] {
  const rules: CssRule[] = [];
  let i = 0;
  while (i < css.length) {
    const open = css.indexOf('{', i);
    if (open === -1) break;
    const selector = css.slice(i, open).trim();
    // find matching close, accounting for nesting
    let depth = 1;
    let j = open + 1;
    while (j < css.length && depth > 0) {
      if (css[j] === '{') depth++;
      else if (css[j] === '}') depth--;
      j++;
    }
    const declarations = css.slice(open + 1, j - 1);
    if (selector) rules.push({ selector, declarations });
    i = j;
  }
  return rules;
}
```

  return parseRules(CSS).filter(r => new RegExp(token).test(r.selector));

```js
/**
 * (New helpers for the search-wrapper / icon / spinner tests below.)
 *
 * `ruleDeclarations` above returns only the FIRST matching rule's block and
 * cannot see inside CSS comments, so it cannot cope with selectors that own
 * more than one rule (e.g. `.clear-btn` has a positioning rule, a base-styling
 * rule, AND a default-hidden `display:none` rule) nor with comma-grouped
 * selectors. The helpers below parse the stylesheet into discrete
 * `{ selector, declarations }` blocks — after stripping comments, since the
 * file's explanatory comments contain literal `{`/`}` (e.g. the
 * `.browser-dialog` block quotes `dialog:not([open]){display:none}`) which
 * would otherwise corrupt naive brace-counting.
 */

/** Remove /* ... *​/ comments (non-greedy, across newlines). */
function stripComments(css: string): string {
  return css.replace(/\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\//g, '');
}

interface CssRule {
  selector: string;
  declarations: string;
}

/**
 * Split a stylesheet into top-level rules, honouring nested braces (so an
 * `@keyframes` body is kept as one rule rather than split at its inner `to {}`).
 */
function parseRules(css: string): CssRule[] {
  const rules: CssRule[] = [];
  let i = 0;
  while (i < css.length) {
    const open = css.indexOf('{', i);
    if (open === -1) break;
    const selector = css.slice(i, open).trim();
    let depth = 1;
    let j = open + 1;
    while (j < css.length && depth > 0) {
      if (css[j] === '{') depth++;
      else if (css[j] === '}') depth--;
      j++;
    }
    const declarations = css.slice(open + 1, j - 1);
    if (selector.length > 0) rules.push({ selector, declarations });
    i = j;
  }
  return rules;
}

const RULES: CssRule[] = parseRules(stripComments(CSS));

/** All rules whose selector contains `selectorToken` (compiled as a regex). */
function rulesMatching(selectorToken: string): CssRule[] {
  const re = new RegExp(selectorToken);
  return RULES.filter((r) => re.test(r.selector));
}

/** True if ANY rule matching `selectorToken` declares something matching `decl`. */
function hasDecl(selectorToken: string, decl: RegExp): boolean {
  return rulesMatching(selectorToken).some((r) => decl.test(r.declarations));
}

/** The first rule matching `selectorToken` that also declares `decl`, or undefined. */
function ruleWith(selectorToken: string, decl: RegExp): CssRule | undefined {
  return rulesMatching(selectorToken).find((r) => decl.test(r.declarations));
}
```
